questions.clue:
[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew reports from the Széchenyi Chain Bridge in Budapest, Hungary.) The Széchenyi Chain Bridge was the first to permanently span this river & connect the two old cities that form Hungary's capital; at the time, there was debate over "Budapest" versus "Pestbuda"

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jimmy of the Clue Crew reports from Nuremberg, Germany.) With some 50 million cookies produced each year in all sizes & shapes, Nuremberg, Germany is this Christmas treat capital of the world

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sarah of the Clue Crew reports from the State Opera House in Vienna, Austria.) The Vienna State Opera House was built in Neo- this style to reflect that 16th century period of artistic rediscovery when the art form of opera was born

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jimmy of the Clue Crew reports from the Great Synagogue in Budapest, Hungary.) The largest synagogue in Europe is found in Budapest & in 1865 this Hungarian composer & keyboard master stopped by to play the organ

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Jimmy of the Clue Crew reports from Mirabell Gardens in Salzburg, Austria.) With beautiful landscaping, fountains & statues, Mirabell Gardens in Salzburg was created in the 1600s but first opened to the public in 1854 by this longtime emperor of Austria
